operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\\n83:  * Trigger}.\\n84:  *\\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\\n90:  *\\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\\n95:  *\\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\\n100:  */\\n101: @Internal\\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\\n105: \\n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\\n107: \\n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n111: \\n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\\n113: \\n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\\n115: \\n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\\n117: \\n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\\n119: \\n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\\n122: \\n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\\n125: \\n126:     /**\\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\\n128:      *\\n129:      * <ul>\\n130:      *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\\n131:      *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\\n132:      *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\\n133:      * </ul>\\n134:      */\\n135:     protected final long allowedLateness;\\n136: \\n137:     /**\\n138:      * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\\n139:      * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\\n140:      * to this.\\n141:      */\\n142:     protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\\n143: \\n144:     private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \\\"numLateRecordsDropped\\\";\\n145: \\n146:     protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\\n147: \\n148:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n149:     // State that is not checkpointed\\n150:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n151: \\n152:     /** The state in which the window contents is stored. ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n179: \\n180:     protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\\n181: \\n182:     /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\\n183:     public WindowOperator(\\n184:             WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\\n185:             TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\\n186:             KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\\n187:             TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\\n188:             StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\\n189:             InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\\n190:             Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\\n191: operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\\n83:  * Trigger}.\\n84:  *\\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\\n90:  *\\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\\n95:  *\\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\\n100:  */\\n101: @Internal\\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\\n105: \\n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\\n107: \\n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n111: \\n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\\n113: \\n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\\n115: \\n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\\n117: \\n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\\n119: \\n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\\n122: \\n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\\n125: \\n126:     /**\\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements.      } else {\\n444:                 this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\\n445:             }\\n446:         }\\n447:     }\\n448: \\n449:     @Override\\n450:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\\n451:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\\n452:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\\n453: \\n454: java.util.Collection;\n77: \n78: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\n79: import static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n80: \n81: /**\n82:  * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n83:  * Trigger}.\n84:  *\n85:  * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n86:  * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n87:  * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n88:  * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n89:  * WindowAssigner}.\n90:  *\n91:  * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n92:  * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n93:  * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n94:  * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n95:  *\n96:  * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n97:  * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n98:  * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n99:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n100:  */\n101: @Internal\n102: public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n103:         extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n104:         implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n105: \n106:     private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n107: \n108:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n109:     // Configuration values and user functions\n110:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n111: \n112:     protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n113: \n114:     private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n115: \n116:     private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n117: \n118:     private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n119: \n120:     /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n121:     protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n122: \n123:     /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n124:     protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n125: \n126:     /**\n127:      * The allowed lateness for elements. @Override\n497:     public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n498:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n499:         triggerContext.window = @Override\n544:     public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n545:         boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n546:                 windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n547: object.\n559:      */\n560:     private void clearAllState(\n561:             W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n562:             throws Exception /**\n592:      * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n593:      * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n594:      *\n595:      * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n596:      * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n597:      */\n598:     protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n599:         @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n600: mergingSetsState);\n603:     }\n604: \n605:     /**\n606:      * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n607:      * the given window.\n608:      */\n609:     protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n610:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n611:                 && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n612:     }\n613: \n614:     /**\n615:      * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n616:      *\n617:      * @param element The element to check\n618:      * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n619:      */\n620:     protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n621:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n622:                 && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n623:                         <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n624:     }\n625: \n626:     /**\n627:      * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n628:      *\n629:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\n630:      */\n631:     protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n632:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n633:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n634:             // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n635:             return;\n636:         }\n637: \n638:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n639:             triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n640:         } else {\n641:             triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n642:         }\n643:     }\n644: \n645:     /**\n646:      * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n647:      *\n648:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\n649:      */\n650:     protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n651:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n652:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n653:             // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n654:             return;\n655:         }\n656:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n657:             triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n658:         } else {\n659:             triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n660:         }\n661:     }\n662: \n663:     /**\n664:      * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n665:      * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n666:      * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n667:      *\n668:      * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n669:      */\n670:     private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n671:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n672:             long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n673:             return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n674:         } else {\n675:             return window.maxTimestamp();\n676:         }\n677:     }\n678: \n679:     /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n680:     protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n681:         return time == cleanupTime(window);\n682:     }\n683: \n684:     /**\n685:      * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. But for merging windows, an element could arrive after the watermark and still be eligible to merge into an existing window that hasn't been cleaned up yet.\n\nLet me check the `isWindowLate()` and `isElementLate()` methods:\n\n```java\nprotected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n    return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n            && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n}\n\nprotected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n    return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n            && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                    <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n}\n```\n\nThe `isWindowLate()` method checks if the window's cleanup time has passed the current watermark, while `isElementLate()` compares the element's timestamp plus allowed lateness against the watermark. The distinction matters for session windows where an element arriving after the watermark might still be valid for merging with existing windows that haven't been cleaned up.\n\nFor merging window assigners, there's a potential issue: when an element arrives, the code checks if the resulting window is late before processing, but doesn't account for whether the element itself is late. An element could be rejected as late even though it could validly merge with an existing window that hasn't exceeded its cleanup time yet.\n\nIn non-merging cases, the logic is straightforward—windows are checked for lateness and skipped if they've passed their deadline. However, the merging path has additional complexity because windows can combine, and the timing of when a window becomes \"late\" versus when an element is considered \"late\" might not align properly. For merging windows, this element could have been merged into an existing window that's still active. The `sideOutput()` method at lines 587-589 calls `output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element)`, which should work if the tag is properly registered, but there may be an issue with how the tag is being handled in this scenario.\n\nLooking more carefully at the logic: the operator checks lateness before merging completes, so an element arriving after the watermark but capable of extending a session window gets incorrectly marked as late. The element then skips the `isSkippedElement = false;` assignment, leaving it true. Later, when both `isSkippedElement` and `isElementLate(element)` are true, the element gets dropped entirely rather than being properly side-outputted.\n\nThe core issue is in the window retirement logic around lines 368-372. When a window is determined to be late, the code retires it and continues without ever setting `isSkippedElement = false`, so the flag remains true even though the element was processed. This causes the final side output check to fail because the element appears to have been skipped when it actually wasn't.\n\nThe late data handling at lines 440-446 only triggers if both `isSkippedElement` is true AND the element itself is late. But there's a scenario with merging windows where an element gets assigned to a window, that window is merged with an existing one, and then the merged result is deemed late and dropped. In this case, the element was already incorporated into a window before that window was retired, so emitting it to late data output becomes problematic.\n\nThe core issue is that when windows merge, the merged window might be late even though the original element's assigned window wasn't. The element gets dropped as part of the merged window, then later flagged as late data based on `isElementLate`, which doesn't accurately reflect what happened—the element could have validly joined an existing window before the merge occurred. the existing test cases to understand what's expected. (key2, 1) @ 10000 - This should be sideoutput as late\n    - Window assignment would give [10000, 10003]\n    - Current watermark is 14600\n    - Element timestamp 10000 + lateness 0 = 10000 <= watermark 14600, so isElementLate = true\n    - But could this element merge with an existing window? The only active window at this point is [11600, 14600]\n    - Does [10000, 10003] overlap with [11600, 14600]? No, they don't overlap.\n    - So the element can't be merged into any window, it should be dropped/sideoutput as late\n\nThe real issue is that the current code drops late elements immediately without checking if they could merge with existing windows that haven't been cleaned up yet. Even if an element arrives after the watermark, it might still be mergeable into an active window, and that merge could trigger new window firings.\n\nConsider this scenario: Element E1 at timestamp 100 creates window [100, 103]. Element E2 at 102 merges with it to form [100, 105]. When the watermark reaches 104, element E3 at timestamp 103 is marked as late. However, E3 would be assigned to window [103, 106], which would merge with the existing [100, 105] to create [100, 106]—a window that isn't late yet since 106 + 0 > 104. The bug is that the merging windows code path doesn't properly account for this scenario when deciding whether to drop the element. [100, 103) and [103, 106) don't overlap since the overlap check requires a.end > b.start && b.end > a.start, which evaluates to 103 > 103 && 106 > 100 = false. So no merge occurs, actualWindow becomes [103, 106], and isWindowLate([103, 106]) returns false since 106 + 0 > 104, allowing the element to be added with isSkippedElement set to false.\n\nI should probably examine the failing tests to understand what's actually going wrong rather than continuing to trace through hypothetical scenarios.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":859,"cache_read_input_tokens":58417,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":859},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"196ec56e-5edb-40b4-8d0a-0aa326020043","uuid":"4b28ae55-8d95-4e48-aea7-bbc9870b54a8"}

Elements from the state windows of merged\n42:  * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\n43:  * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\n44:  *\n45:  * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\n46:  * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\n47:  * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\n48:  * of windows and state.\n49:  *\n50:  * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\n51:  *\n52:  * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\n53:  */\n54: public class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\n55: \n56:     private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\n57: \n58:     /**\n59:      * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\n60:      * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\n61:      * prevent costly state juggling.\n62:      */\n63:     private final Map<W, W> mapping;\n64: \n65:     /**\n66:      * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. We use this to decide whether we need\n67:      * to persist any changes to state.\n68:      */\n69:     private final Map<W, W> initialMapping;\n70: \n71:     private final ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state;\n72: \n73:     /** Our window assigner. */\n74:     private final MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner;\n75: \n76:     /** Restores a {@link MergingWindowSet} from the given state. */\n77:     public MergingWindowSet(\n78:             MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner, ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state)\n79:             throws Exception {\n80:         this.windowAssigner = windowAssigner;\n81:         mapping = new HashMap<>();\n82: \n83:         Iterable<Tuple2<W, W>> windowState = state.get();\n84:         if (windowState != null) {\n85:             for (Tuple2<W, W> window : windowState) {\n86:                 mapping.put(window.f0, window.f1);\n87:             }\n88:         }\n89: \n90:         this.state = state;\n91: \n92:         initialMapping = new HashMap<>();\n93:         initialMapping.putAll(mapping);\n94:     }\n95: \n96:     /**\n97:      * Persist the updated mapping to the given state if the mapping changed since initialization.\n98:      */\n99:     public void persist() throws Exception {\n100:         if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\n101: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java%3FL1-100"}}}

At this point, an element that could have been successfully merged into an active window gets sideoutput as late instead of being processed.\n\nThe sequence is: for each assigned window, attempt to add and merge it; if the resulting window isn't late, include the element. Then after all windows are processed, if the element was skipped and marked as late, it gets emitted to the side output. But there's a gap—an element that could extend a session window arrives after the watermark and gets incorrectly flagged as late before the merge logic can save it.\n\nThe real problem appears to be that lateness is determined before merging happens, so an element capable of extending an existing window gets rejected as late prematurely. The side output emission then fails because the output tag is null at that code path. java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n                .build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n     * triggerContext object.\n     */\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n        processContext.window = window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n     *\n     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n     */\n    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n     */\n    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n     * the given window.\n     */\n    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n     *\n     * @param element The element to check\n     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n     */\n    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n            return;\n        }\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n     */\n    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        } else {\n            return window.maxTimestamp();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n     * state access for {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n\n        // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n        // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n        protected W window;\n\n        public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(\n                    keyedStateBackend,\n                    new SerializerFactory() {\n                        @Override\n                        public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n                                TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n                            return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n          this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = The issue is in the merging windows path. When an element arrives and is assigned to windows, we:\n\n1. Call addWindow which merges the assigned window with existing windows\n2. Check if the resulting window is late\n3. If it is late, we retire the window and skip the element\n\nThe bug is that we should NOT skip the element just because the merged window is late. Instead, we should check if the element arrived before the watermark + allowedLateness. But the element has already been assigned to this window through merging. So the element is lost.\n\nThe fix should be: for merging windows, if the resulting window is late but the element itself is not late (relative to when it was assigned), we should still add the element.\n\nActually, I think a simpler fix is: for merging windows, we should NOT retire a window just because it's late. Instead, we should still add the element and let the trigger/timer handle the cleanup. The timer will clean up the window when its cleanup time passes.\n\nWait, but if the window is late, it means the watermark has already passed its cleanup time. So we should have already cleaned it up.\n\nLet me think about this more carefully:\n- isWindowLate(window) returns true if cleanupTime <= currentWatermark\n- cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness\n\nSo if the window is late, it means the cleanup timer should have already fired or will fire immediately.\n\nBut the element is arriving AFTER that time. So should it be added to the window?\n\nAccording to the Flink semantics, yes! Allowed lateness allows elements to arrive after the watermark and still be added to windows. So if an element arrives with timestamp T, and T + allowedLateness <= watermark, then it's \"late\" and should be sideoutput. But if T + allowedLateness > watermark, then it's NOT late and should be added to any windows it's assigned to.\n\n\nSo the fix is: for merging windows, I should check if the element itself is late, not whether the window is late. If the element hasn't exceeded its allowed lateness window, it should be added even if the window's cleanup time has passed. But once the state for a window has been cleaned up after its cleanup timer fires, we can't add elements to it anymore.\n\nThe key insight from the issue is that an element arriving after the watermark can still extend a session window by increasing its max timestamp and cleanup time. When merging windows, I'm checking if the result window is late, but I should be checking if the original assigned window is late instead. For example, if element E arrives at 105 with watermark 110, gets assigned to window [105, 108], then merges with [100, 108], I need to validate against the original assignment, not the merged result.\n\nBut actually, the element itself has timestamp 105, so it would be marked as late anyway. Let me reconsider what the actual bug is by examining the issue more carefully—it mentions that late data should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` but is being silently dropped instead in the merging window operator.\n\nThe problem appears to be in how `processElement()` handles lateness checking before the merge occurs. An element arriving after the watermark but capable of extending a session window gets incorrectly flagged as late, and then the side output never happens because the output tag becomes unavailable at that code path. Looking at line 442, the side output only occurs if `lateDataOutputTag` is not null, so either the tag is null or we're not reaching that code at all.\n\nRe-examining the merge function itself—there's no side output call within it, so the issue must be elsewhere. The real problem is that for merging windows, late-arriving elements that could still be merged into existing windows are being dropped entirely. The fix should allow these elements to attempt merging even when they arrive after the watermark, rather than rejecting them outright based on lateness.\n\nLooking at the code flow, element lateness is actually checked at the end (line 440), not immediately. The window lateness check happens first (line 369). So the bug lies in how we handle windows that are marked as late—perhaps we shouldn't retire them immediately, but instead skip adding the element and let the final lateness logic determine the outcome.\n\nThe simplest approach might be to remove the window retirement when it's late and just skip that assignment. However, this could leave windows in memory indefinitely. A better strategy would be to check whether the element can merge into an existing non-late window before evaluating the merged result's lateness. Or, ensure that when merging with existing windows, we still add the element to the merged window regardless of its late status.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10368,"cache_read_input_tokens":101856,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10368},"output_tokens":4,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"196ec56e-5edb-40b4-8d0a-0aa326020043","uuid":"4f51b966-4502-47d7-a90e-9e7f52088e55"}
